titleStmt.title: SII 29.35: original edition by P. N. Narasimha Murthy
No. 35. (A.R. No. 93 of 1961-62.) KOLANUPĀKA, BHUVANAGIRI TALUK, NALAGONDA DISTRICT.

summary.p: On a pillar standing near a stream called Peddavāgu.
Mentions the rule of Chālukya Trilōkyamalladēva who was then ruling the empire from Samparavāḍi. mahāsāmanta Mēḷarasa was then governing Koḷḷipāke-7000 an administrative division.

It states that earlier Śaṅkaragaṇḍa of the Rāshṭrakūṭa family had constructed basadis at Rāvunḍi and Nērila and made grants of some lands to them in Juvvikumṭe and Niduṅgalūru by issuing a copper-plate charter. Later on these lands were alienated from the basadis by the queen at the request of mahāsandhivigrahi daṇḍanāyaka Kēsimayya. Subsequently, the record states, that the land grants were restored to the same basadis by the Karaṇas headed by perggaḍe Appaṇayya and Muṁnavapura Venkaṭai-seṭṭī on the occasion of a solar eclipse. Perhaps the Jina Bhavana of Śānti tīrthēśvara, was completely renovated to suit the needs of it being a sacred center. Kēsirāja is mentioned at the end portion of the record which is damaged. Probably he had some role in the renovation of the basadi.
It is dated Śaka 989, Plavaṁga, Kārttika, śu. 13 Monday, corresponding to 1067 A.D., October 23, f.d.t. 20 [Tuesday].

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
bhadramastu jinaśāsanāya . svasti samasta bhu
vanāśraya śrī pṛthvīvallabha mahārājādhi
rāja paramēśvara parama bhaṭṭārakaṁ satyā
śraya kuḷatiḷakaṁ cāḷukyābharaṇaṁ śrīmatrai
lōkyamalladēvara vijayarājya muttarōttarābhi
vṛddhi pravardhamāna mācandrārkka tāraṁ saluttami
re saṁparavāḍiya nelevīḍinoḷsukha saṁkathāvinō
dadiṁ rājyaṁgeyyuttamire tatpāda padmōpajīvi
sāmantaṁ mēḻarasa koḷḷipāke yecchāsiramaṁnāḷu
ttamire sakavarṣa 989 neya plavaṁga saṁvatsarada
kārttika suddha 13 sōmavāra māvara perggaḍe
Appaṇayya pramukha karaṇa muṁnavapura
veṁkaṭaiseṭṭiyu viḻdu māḍida maryyādida
ntena rāṣṭrakūṭa kuḷatiḷakaṁ śrīmanmahā sāmantaṁ śaṁ
karagaṇḍaṁ rāvunḍiyā basadigaḷaṁ neṟila basadige
yumarmmāḍi Isi cakriyiṁ paḍedā tāṁbra śāsanaṁ sa
rvvanamasyaṁ biṭṭa taḷavṛtti juvvikuṁṭṭeyuṁ purada
kēriyuṁ niḍuṁgalūra vāṟaleya tōṁṭamumaṁ pala
vaṁkāla māḍuttami(va)lēyāḷanyāyadiṁ tappi
kupaṇa māḍādalliyācāryyaṁ ka(pi)
samasta dharmmāṟēvakappa mahāhari
sandhivigrahi daṇḍanāyakaṁ kēsimayyaṁgaḷa
(nicna visaliḍi)darasiyar ya
Aṁde bāḷkuḷiya svayaṁbhū
(vidāpu) rupaviḻdu

sūryyagrahaṇa nimittaṁ śrīmatraiḷōkyamalladēvarandu
samasta pradhānaṁ maḷāpakadiṁ śāsana maryyāde sa
rvvanamasyamaṁ kāḷlucci kuḷaleka siṁtivaḷārappandaḷu bahadubandu
navapurada puduvanoldavargganiraṁ nibandhadiṁ
varṣakke taṟuvu gadyāṇa 50 tadoḷaṁ gadyāṇa 5
maṁ koṇḍu tamagarasara dēsiyoḷsaṁ(da)ntappa
bādhegaḷādoḍaṁ kūḷaṁ modalāgi (ḷisa)
lāsthānamaṁ sarvvabādhā parihāraṁ kūcige ba
ḷiyā vantāvakramadiṁ salini lēsalisuva
maryyādadiṁ tappidare ppoḷaśra samaya
dāṇi śrī ballavarana rāṇi piriyarasiya rā
ṇi bāṇarāsiyoḷicchāsira kavilaigaḷa na
ḷida pātakarinnalliyā yā sthānadoḷage
juvvu kuṁṭiya karmmaṭada kanakasamudra
da keṟeya pogaṁṭernneladoḷage moda
ḷāsyāra dānakke nuttarppattu ṁge
mattareraḍu dēva karmma ge mattarondu
dīvige mattarondu paṟakāṟarige
mattannalkātaṁge (ūra) kēriya manide(ṟa)
yu maṁgaraṁga tōṁṭagaḷa komaraga
ṭṭiṁ pākaṁvavarige mattarnnālku mālagāṟage
mattareraḍu ḍamaradavarirvvarige lmattareraḍu

yari
nbārigaṁ mattaṟpadināṟu sige rājaṁge
mattareraḍu hāni vṛddhi
yadadiṁdā sthānamaṁ kāduṇbamudugaṇige
mattarnnālku karaṁba mattarnnālvattu
delliṁ nālku basadigaḷa khaṇḍasphuṭita navaka
rmmaḷādigaḷidu puraṁgaḷi dattiya
velli kālaṁ pratipāḷisuvarendu kāṁmāvi
nāmaṁgaṇa gacchada vaṇananandi maryyādeyaṁ
tappi sadiṁ rājagāmityageydalasya
dā
(syāna gardde pā)
ballavarasa rāṇi . svadattāṁ paradattāṁ vā
yō harēti vasuṁdharā
jāyatē krimiḥ
Ā śvaryyadi mārgga barayisi dha
rmmaṁgaḷaṁ nāśyataṁ māppa śānti tīrtthe
śvara jina bhavanaṁ tīrtthaveṁbantiralmā
natīta
nāṇaṁ śa śrībhavanaṁ śrī kēsirājarana
nesuva māḍidanu prasiddhaṁ .
Digital edition of SII 29.35 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
